selection_rationale: It is a complete 29-shot, 180-second candidate with an explicit prologue and four timed acts.
Its declared act durations permit exact, non-overlapping provisional timeline coverage without estimating per-shot durations.
Completeness and timing make it suitable for partition analysis but do not establish approval, recency, or Gold authority.
authority.approval_record: User approved the default Ledger-Set package: the complete 29-shot candidate, five-group partition, all three modalities per group, and unresolved conflicts retained as scored alternatives rather than inferred canon.

boundary_relevance: The catalog confirms repeated generation, reference, revision, retry, stale-output, and downstream-consumer branches that require bounded rerun units.
The script's fantasy battle and domestic reveal require materially different identity, lighting, scale, sound, and assembly packages.
The repair payoff has an isolated revision target, so it can stop at a boundary after the reality mapping is approved.
production_evidence_assessment.mapping_limit: The snapshot does not prove which production branches belong to the 29-shot candidate; Task 6 must attach only source-backed joins and retain missing joins as unresolved.

groups.narrative_purpose: Establish the failed frontier, the approaching mechanical threat, and the transition from darkness into the guardian's point of view.

atomic_beats.summary: A blackout establishes the failed warning state and distant machine motif.
Fragmentary evidence conveys prior destruction and an overwhelmed defense.
The threat closes in and visually hands off to the guardian.

groups.terminal_artifact: Approved threat-establishment sequence ending on the guardian handoff
groups.smallest_sufficient_rerun: Threat sequence and its transition only

groups.narrative_purpose: Introduce the guardian, scout, attendants, and tiger; communicate the warning; and complete the decision to respond.

atomic_beats.summary: The guardian awakens and sees the threat reflected at close range.
The guardian's full defensive stance and frontier position are established.
The wounded scout reports the failed defenses and imminent attack.
The guardian silently commits and draws the weapon.
Attention shifts toward the supporting force in darkness.
The two attendants intensify the mobilization through the drum rhythm.
The tiger arrives and confirms the guardian-companion partnership.

unresolved: tiger design and name differ in later revisions

production_cohesion: Common frontier lighting and wind state
groups.terminal_artifact: Approved mobilization sequence with all battle participants staged
groups.smallest_sufficient_rerun: Mobilization sequence while preserving the threat package

groups.narrative_purpose: Execute the guardian-and-tiger charge, resolve or interrupt the collision, and carry the damaged guardian into loss of fantasy consciousness.

atomic_beats.summary: The guardian mounts the tiger as the frontier fails.
The scout attempts a final warning.
Guardian and tiger commit to the charge.
The complete candidate shows the strike disabling the threat.
The guardian falls damaged and consciousness cuts to black.

unresolved: some battle branches cut before collision

production_cohesion: Failure recovery can stop before the reality reveal
groups.terminal_artifact: Approved battle outcome and blackout transition
groups.smallest_sufficient_rerun: Charge-through-blackout subgraph, retaining approved mobilization assets

groups.narrative_purpose: Change reality layer, reveal the household scale, map fantasy figures to objects, and establish the family's interpretation of the damage.

atomic_beats.summary: Warm domestic light and ambience replace the fantasy battle.
A child's hand lifts the damaged guardian.
The guardian's point of view reveals the household scale.
Fantasy companions and locations map to household objects.
The child presents the damaged figure to the elder.
The elder identifies the figure and its physical damage.
The child reframes the damage as evidence of heroism.

required_state: guardian and tiger positions available for object mapping

outgoing_continuity.mapping_state: fantasy identities mapped to household objects
unresolved: early black-tiger mapping versus revised natural-tiger mapping

production_cohesion: Shared warm domestic lighting and scale
Object mapping must remain cohesive to preserve the reversal
groups.terminal_artifact: Approved domestic reveal with complete object mapping and damage recognition
groups.smallest_sufficient_rerun: Reality reveal and mapping sequence only

groups.narrative_purpose: Resolve the guardian's identity crisis through repair, renewed protective purpose, and the final title image.

atomic_beats.summary: Warm light holds on the damaged guardian.
The guardian confronts the domestic truth.
The child repairs the damaged area with a symbolic mark.
The child restores the guardian to a protective position.
The guardian's expression is reinterpreted through warmth and relationship.
The guardian accepts a renewed purpose.
The project resolves on a final guardian image and title.

production_cohesion: Repair and emotional reinterpretation are one payoff
groups.terminal_artifact: Approved repair, renewed guardianship, and final title sequence
groups.smallest_sufficient_rerun: Restoration sequence only, preserving the approved reveal

evidence: The timed prologue ends after the threat closes in.
The next act begins on the guardian and expands into cast mobilization.
boundaries.revision_isolation: Threat exposition can be revised without restaging the mobilized cast if the handoff remains intact.
boundaries.smallest_rerun: Either side of the guardian-eye transition plus the transition validation

evidence: The first act completes cast setup and commitment.
The second act begins the high-motion charge and damage sequence.
boundaries.revision_isolation: Battle motion and outcome can be revised after cast setup is approved.
boundaries.smallest_rerun: Charge group and its incoming continuity check
unresolved: Tiger design and battle framing vary across branches.

evidence: The battle act ends in blackout.
The next act changes scale, location meaning, palette, lighting, ambience, and object semantics together.
boundaries.revision_isolation: Fantasy battle variants can stop at the blackout if they preserve the damage and mapping handoff.
boundaries.smallest_rerun: Boundary transition and the affected adjacent group
unresolved: The battle branch may show impact or cut before collision.

evidence: The third act completes the domestic mapping and heroic reinterpretation.
The fourth act changes the objective from understanding damage to repairing and honoring it.
boundaries.revision_isolation: The repair symbol and final emotional beat can change without rebuilding the reality reveal.
boundaries.smallest_rerun: Restoration group only unless the approved symbol requires an earlier setup change
unresolved: Heart and five-point-star repair symbols compete.
Human visibility constraints may apply to both groups.

alternative_partitions.difference: Split the first timed act between threat assessment and the scout warning so discovery and response have separate revision scopes.
alternative_partitions.blocking_evidence: The source gives a 45-second act duration but no per-shot timing for shots 4-10, so an exact time boundary cannot be recorded without inference.

alternative_partitions.difference: Combine threat with mobilization and combine reveal with restoration.
alternative_partitions.blocking_evidence: The combined groups contain independently revisable objectives and media packages.
Most cuts lack an independent objective, production package, terminal artifact, or revision benefit.

alternative_partitions.difference: Use another complete screenplay or shot lineage as the master before applying the same boundary rules.
alternative_partitions.blocking_evidence: No approval flag or final-master identifier establishes which complete lineage owns Gold semantics.

unresolved_decisions.question: Which complete script or shot lineage is approved as the master?
Should the first timed act remain one group or split after threat assessment once shot timing is approved?
Which tiger design and identity are authoritative?
Should the collision and impact be shown or should the sequence cut before impact?
Does the no-identifiable-face rule apply project-wide or only to late-story branches?
Is the repair symbol a heart, a five-point star, or another approved mark?
Which camera specifications and music timings are binding rather than experimental?

verification.approval_policy: Master semantics and partition are approved. Unresolved implementation choices remain measurable alternatives and may not be silently promoted to exact Gold.
